channel.title: The Grant Desk - Cross-disciplinary & Social Impact, Asia
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Cross-disciplinary & Social Impact, Asia.

item.title: A4 Residency: 2027-2028 International Residency Program (Chengdu)
item.description:
Organisation: A4 Residency Art Center (A4 Art Museum)

Location: Chengdu, China

Award: Two tracks. Fully Funded Fellowship: private studio and accommodation, round-trip economy airfare or 2nd-class train (within a set budget), and an RMB 10,000 production grant, plus resource networking, media exposure and presentation opportunities. Light Version: self-funded (studio and accommodation provided, but the artist covers travel and production costs). No application fee.

Duration: Approx. 8 weeks per cycle (Autumn 2027: Jul-Oct; Spring 2028: Apr-Jun; Autumn 2028: Jul-Oct)

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Multidisciplinary international residency at the A4 Residency Art Center in Chengdu, China, a global exchange platform integrating creation, networking and resources, which has hosted 335 creators from 44+ countries since 2011. Residents engage with the art center, community and city through interdisciplinary, locally-engaged practice, supported by A4's resource library, a 'Super Agent' system pairing artists with local residents, and a documentation/research programme. Two participation tracks: a Fully Funded Fellowship (studio, accommodation, travel within budget, RMB 10,000 production grant, networking, media and presentation support) and a self-funded 'Light Version' (studio and accommodation provided; travel and production self-covered). Residents initiate 1-2 participatory local events and take part in open studios, field trips and sharing events, with outcomes shown via exhibitions, performances, publications or public art. WHO CAN APPLY: multidisciplinary artists (visual, performing, music, film, sound, architecture), curators, designers (product, fashion, spatial, interactive), and interdisciplinary creatives integrating art, business and culture; international, no nationality restriction. DEADLINES: 2027 recruitment by 30 June 2026; 2028 year-round recruitment by 31 December 2026. APPLICATION: submit a personal photo, portfolio, CV and project proposal in both English and Chinese by email (a4residency@a4am.cn for the funded fellowship; qiaojiayi@a4residency.com.cn for the Light Version).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Taiwan AID: 2026 NGO Fellowship Program (Asia and Pacific Islands)
item.description:
Organisation: Taiwan Alliance in International Development (Taiwan AID)

Location: Taipei, Taiwan (fully on-site participation required, including a three-week field placement at a Taiwanese NGO)

Award: Fully funded: round-trip airfare, accommodation (dormitory-style rooms, 6-8 beds per room), meals, and local transportation in Taiwan are all covered. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium.

Duration: Four weeks, 4 October - 1 November 2026

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Taiwan Alliance in International Development (Taiwan AID) annual fellowship for NGO professionals from South Asia, Southeast Asia, Northeast Asia and the Pacific Islands. Approximately 12 fellows selected (tentative) for a four-week immersive programme combining (a) a three-week placement at a Taiwanese NGO (event preparation, project management, fundraising activities), (b) workshops on project management, fundraising and advocacy plus civil society site visits in Taiwan, (c) a roundtable with Taiwanese NGO professionals, and (d) attendance at Taiwan LGBT+ Pride. Fellows propose a project draft for future collaboration with their host NGO at the end of the programme and join an alumni network across Asia. ELIGIBILITY: NGO professionals (community development, environment, education, women's rights, civic engagement and human rights, emergency relief, disability inclusion, children and families welfare, social innovation and entrepreneurship; outstanding applicants from other fields also considered); at least three years of NGO sector experience; aged 25-40; from Southeast Asia (Brunei, Cambodia, Laos, Timor-Leste, Indonesia, Malaysia, Myanmar, the Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, Vietnam), South Asia (Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Maldives, Pakistan), Northeast Asia (Japan, South Korea, Mongolia) or Pacific Islands (Palau, Marshall Islands, Tuvalu); proficient in written and oral English (Mandarin a plus).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
